description: The MSP sign-in server route reads the remembered-email cookie and passes the value into the shared MSP login form.
The shared MSP login form initializes its email field from a provided remembered email value.
The MSP login form renders a `Public workstation - do not remember my email` checkbox.
The public-workstation checkbox defaults to unchecked on every fresh visit to MSP sign-in.
Successful credentials sign-in with public-workstation unchecked stores the normalized email in the durable remembered-email cookie.
Successful credentials sign-in with public-workstation checked clears any existing durable remembered-email cookie.
Failed credentials sign-in leaves durable remembered-email state unchanged.
Remembered email values are normalized by trimming whitespace and converting to lowercase before persistence.
The durable remembered-email cookie uses a 180-day max age.
The durable remembered-email cookie is stored separately from session cookies and existing remembered SSO provider state.
The durable remembered-email cookie is server-readable for MSP sign-in prefill and is not required to be client-readable.
MSP SSO resolve requests include remember-context fields for normalized email and public-workstation preference.
The MSP SSO resolve route persists a short-lived pending remember-context cookie before redirecting to OAuth.
Successful OAuth sign-in with public-workstation unchecked promotes pending remember-context into the durable remembered-email cookie.
Successful OAuth sign-in with public-workstation checked clears any existing durable remembered-email cookie.
OAuth callback handling clears pending remember-context after finalization.
Failed or abandoned OAuth flows do not create or update the durable remembered-email cookie.
Prefilled remembered email still triggers the existing domain-based SSO discovery behavior on the MSP sign-in page.
A later successful MSP sign-in can replace a previously remembered email with a newly successful email when public-workstation is unchecked.
The remembered-email feature applies only to MSP sign-in and does not change client portal login behavior.
